chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P.No.D- 434 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders as to non-prosecution. 02.04.2019. None present. = It appears that on the very first date viz. 01.03.2017, counsel for the petitioner was put on notice to satisfy the Court about the maintainability of instant petition. However, as an indulgence notice was ordered to be issued to the other side for 06.04.2017. On 06.04.2017 again counsel for the petitioner sought time to make compliance of the earlier order. Today the position is same as neither the petitioner nor his counsel is in attendance. It appears that petitioner and his counsel have lost interest in the proceedings or the purpose for which the instant petition was filed has been served therefore, instead to pursue their case vigilantly they have chosen to remain absent. Consequently, petition is dismissed in default alongwith all pending applications. JUDGE JUDGE Tufail/PA
